title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain holds a slim edge in trader consensus at 17.2% implied probability to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup, just ahead of France at 16.6%, reflecting both nations' dominant UEFA qualifying campaigns and the recent final draw that shields top seeds like them, England, and Argentina from early knockout clashes until at least the semifinals. Spain's momentum from Euro 2024 glory and Nations League form, bolstered by young stars like Lamine Yamal, is tempered by France's clinical 2-1 friendly win over Brazil on March 26, showcasing Kylian Mbappé's sharpness amid a deep attacking pool. Defending champions Argentina (8.8%) and Brazil (8.5%) remain competitive via CONMEBOL table-topping finishes, but the expanded 48-team format and North American hosting introduce group stage uncertainties, keeping the race tight among elite squads with proven depth, home/away splits, and stylistic matchups poised for upsets.

eventMetadata.context_description: Paris Saint-Germain FC's 2-0 victory over Liverpool FC in the UEFA Champions League quarter-final second leg at Anfield on April 14 propelled traders to price PSG at a dominant 100% implied probability, completing a comprehensive 4-0 aggregate win after Désiré Doué and Khvicha Kvaratskhelia scored in the first leg six days prior. Ousmane Dembélé's late brace extinguished Liverpool's comeback hopes despite an overturned VAR penalty, while Arne Slot's side struggled with a toothless attack—Alexander Isak subbed at halftime amid harsh critiques—and failed to penetrate PSG's robust defense led by reigning champions' clinical finishing. With the full-time whistle confirmed by official sources, no realistic challenges like disputes or rescissions remain to alter this trader consensus.

title: Military action against Iran ends by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if neither the US nor Israel initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Iranian soil or any official Iranian embassy or consulate on a full calendar day by the listed date, Iran Standard Time (GMT+3:30). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the date/time of a qualifying strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar day after the listed date, the respective market will resolve to "Yes" regardless of whether a strike is later confirmed to have occurred. 

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by US or Israeli military forces that impact Iranian ground territory or any official Iranian embassy or consulate (e.g., if a weapons depot on Iranian soil is hit by an US/Israeli missile, this will qualify).

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be considered, regardless of whether they land on Iranian territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US/Israeli ground operatives will not be considered.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

series.title: Military action against Iran ends by

eventMetadata.context_description: US naval forces initiated a blockade of Iranian ports and the Strait of Hormuz on April 14, 2026, enforcing President Trump's warning against Iranian warships approaching the restricted zone, amid Tehran's rejection of ceasefire proposals to reopen the vital waterway closed since late February. This follows a fragile two-week truce announced April 7 after weeks of intense US-Israeli airstrikes on Iranian missile sites, air defenses, and nuclear facilities, met by Iranian drone and missile retaliations against US bases and allies. Diplomatic efforts in Islamabad faltered on April 12, with no progress on de-escalation, while Hezbollah threats loom; traders watch for blockade enforcement outcomes, Hormuz tanker transits, and truce expiration around April 21, which could trigger renewed airstrikes or peace talks.

title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: California Governor Gavin Newsom leads Polymarket trader consensus for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination at 27.5% implied probability, reflecting his top rankings in recent national polls like Emerson College's March survey and proactive national fundraising amid a wide-open field following the 2024 election loss. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez trails at 8.4% on progressive appeal and youth demographics, while Kamala Harris has faded to 6.1% due to lingering 2024 baggage despite some polling strength. Jon Ossoff at 5.9% gains from Georgia wins and Senate profile, and Josh Shapiro at 4.0% benefits from Pennsylvania swing-state governance. Key differentiators include executive experience for governors like Newsom and Shapiro, versus congressional fresh faces like Ossoff; consolidation could hinge on 2026 midterm performances, FEC fundraising reports, early Iowa and New Hampshire polling, and endorsements from party elders.

title: Club Atlético de Madrid vs. FC Barcelona
description: This event is for the upcoming UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for Tuesday, April 14, 2026 between Club Atlético de Madrid and FC Barcelona.

markets.question: Will Club Atlético de Madrid win on 2026-04-14?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 14, 2026
If Club Atlético de Madrid wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Barcelona's commanding 2-1 victory in the UEFA Champions League quarterfinal second leg at the Wanda Metropolitano has driven trader consensus to a near-certain 100% implied probability on their win, sealing the match outcome after Lamine Yamal and Ferran Torres struck early, with Ademola Lookman pulling one back for Atletico. Despite trailing 0-2 on aggregate from the first leg and finishing with 10 men following a late red card, Barcelona's clinical finishing and defensive resolve in Hansi Flick's high-line setup overcame Diego Simeone's disciplined Colchoneros press. Atletico advances 3-2 overall to the semifinals, but this moneyline market reflects the 90-minute result alone; only an improbable official overturn via VAR or appeal could challenge it now.

title: Peru Presidential Election Winner
description:
General elections are scheduled to be held in Peru on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who wins the next Peruvian Presidential election. 

This market includes any potential second round. 

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the results of this election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve solely on the official results as reported by the Peruvian government, specifically the National Office of Electoral Processes (Oficina Nacional de Procesos Electorales, ONPE) (https://www.onpe.gob.pe/elecciones/) and the National Jury of Elections (Jurado Nacional de Elecciones, JNE) (https://portal.jne.gob.pe/portal/)

eventMetadata.context_description: Keiko Fujimori's 60.5% implied probability to win Peru's presidency stems from her lead in first-round exit polls and ONPE quick counts—around 16-17% with over 75% of actas processed as of April 14—positioning her for the June 7 runoff in a fragmented 35-candidate field where no one neared 50%. Logistical chaos, including ballot shortages extending voting into April 13, delayed full results but solidified her edge amid voter concerns over crime and instability. Roberto Sánchez Palomino's 27.4% reflects his tight second-place contention per Ipsos and Datum flash electorales, bolstered by center-left appeal, while Rafael López Aliaga's 12.5% tracks his conservative third-place showings; a Sánchez-Fujimori runoff favors her historical resilience and right-wing base in past runoffs.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Portland Trail Blazers win the 2026 NBA Finals. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NBA Finals based off the rules of the NBA.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NBA.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder lead trader consensus at 41.5% implied probability for the 2026 NBA championship as defending champions who clinched the Western Conference's No. 1 seed five days ago with their seventh straight win, securing home-court advantage throughout the playoffs amid a league-best 67-14 regular-season record. San Antonio Spurs sit second at 15.2% after a strong campaign fueled by Victor Wembanyama's dominance, though their April 13 loss to Denver halted momentum. Boston Celtics (11.8%) and Nuggets (10.5%) trail closely, with Denver's 12-game winning streak—including a 128-118 rout of San Antonio yesterday—boosting their contention despite recent 127-107 upset over OKC reserves. Eastern leaders like Detroit Pistons (5.9%) face a tougher path against OKC's depth and Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's MVP-caliber play.

title: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?
description: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?

markets.question: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $160 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: WTI crude oil front-month futures plunged over 7% to settle near $92 per barrel on April 14, 2026, as trader sentiment shifted on hopes for US-Iran peace talks easing geopolitical premiums tied to Strait of Hormuz disruptions, extending a 15% pullback from early-month highs above $113. Elevated US crude inventories, up 3.1 million barrels to 464.7 million for the week ended April 3 per latest EIA data, compounded bearish pressures alongside Goldman Sachs' revised Q2 forecast to $87 per barrel amid softer demand outlooks. OPEC+ initiated production adjustments this month, while tomorrow's EIA weekly report and further diplomatic developments loom as key catalysts for volatility through April's end.

title: Counter-Strike: Natus Vincere vs FURIA (BO3) - IEM Rio Group B
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Upper bracket semifinal 1 match between Natus Vincere and FURIA in the IEM Rio Group B, initially scheduled for April 14 at 2:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Natus Vincere" if Natus Vincere win the match against FURIA.

This market will resolve to "FURIA" if FURIA win the match against Natus Vincere.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

"Total combined rounds" is the sum of rounds won by both teams during the match, including any overtime rounds if applicable. For example, a match ending 13–11 would have a total of 24 rounds (even), while a match ending 13–10 would have a total of 23 rounds (odd).

markets.groupItemTitle: Map Handicap: FURIA (-1.5) vs Natus Vincere (+1.5)

eventMetadata.context_description: Natus Vincere, HLTV #2 with a dominant 2-1 group stage win over B8 yesterday highlighted by w0nderful's rifling and b1t's clutches, face FURIA (#7) in IEM Rio 2026 Group B upper bracket semi-final BO3, where the winner advances directly to playoffs. FURIA, buoyed by home crowd energy in Rio, crushed Passion UA 2-0 earlier, leveraging FalleN's IGL and KSCERATO's aggression after recent roster boosts with YEKINDAR and molodoy. NaVi leads head-to-head 13-10, including a recent Krakow loss to FURIA, but fields stand-in makazze amid jL's absence; traders weigh NaVi's superior form and rankings against FURIA's momentum and map veto edges on Mirage, Dust2, or Nuke.

title: Heat vs. Hornets
description:

If the Heat win, the market will resolve to "Heat".
If the Hornets win, the market will resolve to "Hornets".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Charlotte Hornets (44-38) host the Miami Heat (43-39) in the Eastern Conference 9-10 NBA Play-In Tournament at Spectrum Center on April 14, with the winner advancing to face the 8th seed for a playoff spot. Hornets enjoy home-court advantage and recently crushed Miami 136-106 on March 17 amid the Heat's 17-24 road record. Miami's latest injury report confirms Tyler Herro (foot), Andrew Wiggins (toe), and Simone Fontecchio (ankle) available, Pelle Larsson questionable (leg contusion), Nikola Jovic (ankle) and Dru Smith (foot) out; Charlotte reports no significant absences. Recent form shows both scrapping into play-in contention, heightening upset potential in this high-stakes elimination matchup.

title: Fed decision in April?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's April 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders assign a 98.7% implied probability to no change in the federal funds rate at the April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting, aligning with CME FedWatch Tool consensus near 98%, driven by the Fed's March 18 decision to hold the 3.5%-3.75% target amid resilient labor markets and sticky inflation. March CPI accelerated to 3.3% year-over-year from February's 2.4%, propelled by a 12.5% energy index surge tied to geopolitical oil shocks, while FOMC minutes released April 8 highlighted some openness to hikes but affirmed a baseline pause with one 2026 cut projected in the dot plot. Scenarios challenging this include sharply weaker April jobs data or sub-2% core inflation readings, though traders see limited catalysts pre-meeting.

title: Republican Presidential Nominee 2028
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Republican Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Republican Party sources.

Any replacement of the Republican nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Robert F. Kennedy Jr. as the frontrunner for the 2028 Republican presidential nomination at 49%, buoyed by his visibility as HHS Secretary implementing health policy reforms like vaccine scrutiny and nutrition initiatives, appealing to the populist base. Vice President J.D. Vance holds 38.6% amid declining approval ratings—down to 36% GOP support in the latest YouGov poll—stemming from backlash over the Trump administration's escalating Iran conflict, which clashes with his prior anti-forever-war rhetoric. Secretary of State Marco Rubio's 21.8% reflects surging profile from leading diplomatic responses to the Iran crisis, narrowing the gap with Vance. Recent CPAC and Yale Youth polls favor Vance at 43-53%, underscoring market-poll divergence ahead of 2026 midterms.

title: Eurovision Winner 2026
description:
This market will resolve to the country whose candidate for Eurovision 2026 wins.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Finland's duo Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen top trader consensus at 36.7% implied probability to win Eurovision 2026, propelled by their February UMK triumph with "Liekinheitin"—a violin-driven pop anthem blending classical flair and high-energy staging that has dominated OGAE fan polls and early previews. France's Monroe follows at 12% with the operatic "Regarde," gaining from vocal jury appeal post-national selection, while Denmark's Søren Torpegaard sits at 10.4% on the emotive ballad "Før vi går hjem" from his DMGP victory. Nordic televote strength and streaming metrics bolster the leaders amid a crowded field of 35 entries, but jury unpredictability and Vienna rehearsals could spark shifts before semis May 12-14 and final May 16.

title: Colombia Chamber of Representatives Election: 3rd Place
description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Colombia on 8 March 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the third-greatest number of seats in the Colombian Chamber of Representatives as a result of this election.

If the results of this election are not definitively known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid votes received, with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party abbreviations. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the third-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition in the Chamber of Representatives.

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors Partido Liberal Colombiano (PLC) for third place in Colombia's Chamber of Representatives election held March 8, 2026, reflecting official results from the Registraduría Nacional showing PLC securing 26 seats behind Historic Pact for Colombia (PH) at 42 and Centro Democrático (CD) at 30. Final scrutiny completed by March 31 confirmed this distribution, with no successful challenges or recounts altering the top three amid a fragmented field where Partido Conservador Colombiano trails at 19 seats and others like Cambio Radical (CR) and Partido de la U at 12 each. The commanding position stems from PLC's consistent national vote share of around 11-12% across departmental circunscripciones, solidifying its position in this proportional representation system. While probabilities exceed 90%, potential shifts could arise from rare late legal disputes over seat allocations or curules de paz adjustments, though institutional certification processes indicate resolution imminent.

title: Presidential Election Winner 2028
description:
The 2028 US Presidential Election is scheduled to take place on November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance leads trader consensus for the 2028 presidential election at 19.3% implied probability, narrowly ahead of California Governor Gavin Newsom at 17.2% and Secretary of State Marco Rubio at 10.1%, driven by Vance's incumbency edge as Trump's running mate post-2024 victory but tempered by a recent plunge in his polling and prediction market support amid administration scrutiny. Newsom benefits from strong Democratic primary showings in surveys like Emerson's August 2025 poll, bolstered by his national book tour and family deliberations on a post-midterm run. Rubio rises via high-profile foreign policy moves, including Venezuela operations, and CPAC straw poll gains in late March. The tight race reflects the long timeline to November 2028 primaries, open fields on both sides, and pivotal 2026 midterms that could dictate party control, momentum in swing states, and Trump endorsements—strong midterm performances or policy wins could create separation.

title: Counter-Strike: Spirit vs Team Falcons (BO3) - IEM Rio Group A
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Upper bracket semifinal 2 match between Spirit and Team Falcons in the IEM Rio Group A, initially scheduled for April 14 at 11:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Spirit" if Spirit win the match against Team Falcons.

This market will resolve to "Team Falcons" if Team Falcons win the match against Spirit.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Map Handicap: TS (-1.5) vs Team Falcons (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Upper bracket semifinal 2 match between Spirit and Team Falcons in the IEM Rio Group A, initially scheduled for April 14 at 12:40PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Spirit" if Spirit wins 2 or more maps than Team Falcons in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Team Falcons". 

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Rounds?

"Total combined rounds" is the sum of rounds won by both teams during the match, including any overtime rounds if applicable. For example, a match ending 13–11 would have a total of 24 rounds (even), while a match ending 13–10 would have a total of 23 rounds (odd).

eventMetadata.context_description: Team Spirit and Team Falcons clashed in a decisive BO3 Group A matchup at IEM Rio 2026, with the winner advancing directly to playoffs semifinals amid double-elimination format. Both squads dominated openers yesterday—Spirit sweeping Liquid 2-0 on solid Anubis play, Falcons dismantling 3DMAX 2-0 via aggressive executes—highlighting strong group stage momentum. Falcons hold trader edge as top-5 HLTV side with NiKo-led firepower and fresh karrigan addition for IGL stability, contrasting Spirit's #9 ranking and 3-2 recent form marked by donk's frags but role inconsistencies. Head-to-head leans Falcons (Spirit's lone win outdated); map pool vetoes like Inferno/Overpass bans and Anubis pick pivotal in closely contested odds reflecting stylistic clash.

title: What will Trump post this week? (April 6 - April 12)
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if @realDonaldTrump posts/truths the listed term between April 6, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and April 12, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, all text posted by the listed account in quote and reply posts/truths count toward a "Yes" resolution, but quoted posts/truths and reposts/reTruths will not count.

Text posted in images, memes, or other non-animated, non-video media that are not strictly text will qualify towards a "Yes" resolution only if the listed term is spelled out clearly and in full. (e.g., words spelled out in a letter posted as a .jpg will qualify, however a word posted as part of an animated .gif will not.)

Any plural or possessive forms of a listed term, as well as variance in capitalizations, will count toward the resolution of this market, regardless of context. Other forms of the listed term will NOT count.

Extraneous symbols being inserted into a word (ex: r@d1cal, for "radical") will disqualify it from counting toward a "Yes" resolution.

Misspellings or iterations of the listed term, including all grammatical or slang forms, or misspellings with extra, missing, or incorrect letters (ex: helloooooooo or heoll, for ‘hello’), will not count toward a “Yes” resolution, regardless of context or intent.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

The resolution source for this market will be Donald Trumps's verified Truth Social account: @realDonaldTrump

Please note, only the @realDonaldTrump verified Truth Social account counts for this market, regardless of the URL for this profile. If Donald Trump posts/truths from another account, it has no bearing on the resolution of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Donald Trump's prolific Truth Social activity, often multiple posts daily, centers on foreign policy critiques, personal vindication, and sharp retorts to media or adversaries, shaping trader expectations for the April 6-12 window. Escalating U.S.-Iran tensions dominated early-week sentiment after a U.S. rescue of a colonel prompted Trump's Easter Sunday expletive-laden threats of strikes on Iranian power plants and bridges. Mid-week, a 60 Minutes report featuring Catholic cardinals criticizing his social media fueled attacks on Pope Leo XIV as "weak on crime" and poor on foreign policy. Culminating April 12, Trump shared then deleted an AI image portraying himself in Christ-like garb healing the sick, drawing widespread backlash from religious leaders and politicians. No confirmed resolution triggers beyond verifiable Truth Social posts during the period.

title: UEFA Champions League Winner
description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will win the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League (soccer).

markets.question: Will Real Madrid win the 2025–26 Champions League?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on whether the listed team will win the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team is officially crowned the winner of the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to win the tournament (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from UEFA Champions League (https://www.uefa.com/uefachampionsleague/). A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team is officially crowned the winner of the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to win the tournament (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from UEFA Champions League (https://www.uefa.com/uefachampionsleague/). A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Bayern Munich (29.5%), Arsenal (28.5%), and PSG (26.5%) in a tight cluster for UEFA Champions League winner, reflecting the razor-thin margins in quarterfinal ties and recent advancements amid the knockout phase's unpredictability. PSG sealed semifinal progression with a crucial second-leg victory at Anfield over Liverpool (2-0 first leg), showcasing clinical finishing, while Atlético Madrid clung to a 3-2 aggregate edge past 10-man Barcelona despite a 2-1 second-leg loss at home. Bayern holds a favorable 2-1 advantage from their Santiago Bernabéu triumph over Real Madrid ahead of tomorrow's Allianz Arena decider, bolstered by Harry Kane's form. Arsenal's 1-0 away win versus Sporting faces scrutiny with Saka and Rice injury concerns, yet their defensive record keeps the race fiercely contested entering semifinals.

title: Busan: Yi Zhou vs Pavel Kotov
description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Yi Zhou and Pavel Kotov in the Busan, scheduled for April 12 at 9:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Yi Zhou' if Yi Zhou advances against Pavel Kotov.

This market will resolve to 'Pavel Kotov' if Pavel Kotov advances against Yi Zhou.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Zhou vs. Kotov: Set 1 Games O/U 8.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Yi Zhou and Pavel Kotov in the Busan, scheduled for April 13 2026.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed in the first set equals or exceeds 9. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
Any first-set tiebreak counts as one (1) game toward the total.
If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins, or delayed beyond 7 days without commencement, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official Challenger results.
markets.groupItemTitle: Busan: Yi Zhou vs Pavel Kotov Set 1 O/U 8.5

markets.question: Yi Zhou vs. Pavel Kotov: Total Sets O/U 2.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Yi Zhou and Pavel Kotov in the Busan, scheduled for April 13 2026.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of sets completed equals or exceeds 3. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
A super tie breaker is considered as one (1) set for total set markets.
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official Challenger statistics.

markets.question: Zhou vs. Kotov: Match O/U 22.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Yi Zhou and Pavel Kotov in the Busan, scheduled for April 13 2026.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed across all sets equals or exceeds 23. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
All tiebreaks—including any Champions/Super tiebreak—count as one (1) game toward the total.

markets.question: Set 1 Winner: Zhou vs Kotov
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Yi Zhou and Pavel Kotov in the Busan, scheduled for April 13 2026.
This market will resolve to “Zhou” if Yi Zhou wins the first set. It will resolve to “Kotov” if Pavel Kotov wins the first set.
If the match begins but is not completed, and the first set is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on that completed set. If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Pavel Kotov enters the Busan Challenger first-round clash on hard courts as the slight favorite over wildcard Yi Zhou, buoyed by a 1-0 head-to-head edge from their hard-court meeting at the 2024 China Open where Kotov rallied for a three-set win. Kotov arrives in strong form after a semifinal run at the prior week's Wuning Challenger, defeating players like Fajing Sun and Ilia Simakin in gritty three-setters, showcasing improved stamina and baseline aggression. Zhou, ranked around No. 220, has shown flashes but lacks Kotov's recent momentum on the surface, with no major injury concerns reported pre-match. The evenly matched rankings suggest upset potential, hinging on serve holds and return pressure under Busan conditions.

title: F1 Drivers' Champion
description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' superior constructor performance has propelled teammates George Russell and Kimi Antonelli to the top of trader consensus for the 2026 F1 Drivers' Championship, with Russell's 43.5% implied probability reflecting his experience edge over the 19-year-old Antonelli at 30.5%, despite the Italian leading standings 72-63 after three Grands Prix. Antonelli's victory at the Japanese Grand Prix on March 29—his second win after China—overtook Russell for the points lead, extending Mercedes' podium sweep from Australia onward amid Red Bull's development struggles sidelining Max Verstappen to 2.4%. Leclerc trails at 49 points for Ferrari but faces a steep constructors' gap, underscoring the internal Mercedes title battle as the key dynamic.

title: Dota 2: Team Nemesis vs REKONIX (BO3) - DreamLeague Southeast Asia Closed Qualifier Playoffs
description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 Lower bracket final match between Team Nemesis and REKONIX in the DreamLeague Southeast Asia Closed Qualifier Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 14 at 12:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Team Nemesis" if Team Nemesis win the match against REKONIX.

This market will resolve to "REKONIX" if REKONIX win the match against Team Nemesis.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Total Kills Over/Under 47.5 in Game 2?
markets.description:
In the upcoming Lower bracket final match between Team Nemesis and REKONIX in the DreamLeague Southeast Asia Closed Qualifier Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 14 at 12:00AM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 2.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 2 is 48 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.


In Dota 2, a "kill" for markets like team total kills, kill handicap, and interval kills is counted when an enemy hero dies and the game awards a hero kill event to the opposing team's score, which is the number shown on the in-game scoreboard and tracked in scoreboard feeds as team kills. In practical terms, this includes any enemy hero death that increments a team's kill score, regardless of who got the last hit, including situations where the victim dies to creeps, a tower, or neutrals as long as the kill is still credited to the enemy team. It does not include denies, such as killing your own allied units, and it also does not include building or objective destructions like towers, barracks, or Roshan, since those are tracked separately from kills. Assists also do not count as kills, because they are recorded as a separate player stat and multiple players can assist on a single kill.

markets.question: Game 1: Ends in Daytime?
markets.description:
This market is about whether Game 1 ends during the daytime phase of the Dota 2 day/night cycle. In Dota 2, the day/night cycle alternates every 5 minutes (daytime runs from 0:00–5:00, 10:00–15:00, 20:00–25:00, and so on, while nighttime runs from 5:00–10:00, 15:00–20:00, 25:00–30:00, and so on).

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the game-ending action (Ancient destruction or midgame forfeit) occurs during a daytime phase of the base in-game clock. This market will resolve to "No" if the game ends during a nighttime phase. Temporary nighttime effects caused by hero abilities (e.g., Night Stalker's Dark Ascension) do not affect resolution, only the base day/night cycle timer is considered.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Beat Roshan?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams kill Roshan at least once during Game 1. Roshan is a powerful neutral boss in Dota 2 that spawns at the start of the game and respawns 8–11 minutes after each death, dropping the Aegis of the Immortal and other valuable items.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Team Nemesis and REKONIX each kill Roshan at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not kill Roshan during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via forfeit, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had killed Roshan prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Destroy Barracks?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams destroy at least one enemy barracks during Game 1. In Dota 2, each team has six barracks (a melee and ranged barracks in each of the three lanes), located behind the Tier 3 towers.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Team Nemesis and REKONIX each destroy at least one enemy barracks (melee or ranged) during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) do not destroy any enemy barracks.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

markets.question: Game 1: Any Player Ultra Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves an Ultra Kill during Game 1. In Dota 2, an Ultra Kill occurs when a single player kills 4 enemy heroes in rapid succession without dying.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves an Ultra Kill during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves an Ultra Kill. A Rampage (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as an Ultra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In Dota 2, a "kill" for markets like team total kills, kill handicap, and interval kills is counted when an enemy hero dies and the game awards a hero kill event to the opposing team's score, which is the number shown on the in-game scoreboard and tracked in scoreboard feeds as team kills. In practical terms, this includes any enemy hero death that increments a team's kill score, regardless of who got the last hit, including situations where the victim dies to creeps, a tower, or neutrals as long as the kill is still credited to the enemy team. It does not include denies, such as killing your own allied units, and it also does not include building or objective destructions like towers, barracks, or Roshan, since those are tracked separately from kills. Assists also do not count as kills, because they are recorded as a separate player stat and multiple players can assist on a single kill.

markets.question: Game 2: Ends in Daytime?
markets.description:
This market is about whether Game 2 ends during the daytime phase of the Dota 2 day/night cycle. In Dota 2, the day/night cycle alternates every 5 minutes (daytime runs from 0:00–5:00, 10:00–15:00, 20:00–25:00, and so on, while nighttime runs from 5:00–10:00, 15:00–20:00, 25:00–30:00, and so on).

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the game-ending action (Ancient destruction or midgame forfeit) occurs during a daytime phase of the base in-game clock. This market will resolve to "No" if the game ends during a nighttime phase. Temporary nighttime effects caused by hero abilities (e.g., Night Stalker's Dark Ascension) do not affect resolution, only the base day/night cycle timer is considered.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 2: Both Teams Beat Roshan?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams kill Roshan at least once during Game 2. Roshan is a powerful neutral boss in Dota 2 that spawns at the start of the game and respawns 8–11 minutes after each death, dropping the Aegis of the Immortal and other valuable items.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Team Nemesis and REKONIX each kill Roshan at least once during Game 2. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not kill Roshan during Game 2.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed and ends via forfeit, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had killed Roshan prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 3: Any Player Ultra Kill?
markets.description:

markets.question: Game 3: Ends in Daytime?
markets.description:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the game-ending action (Ancient destruction or midgame forfeit) occurs during a daytime phase of the base in-game clock. eventMetadata.context_description: In the double-elimination bracket of DreamLeague Season 29 Southeast Asia Closed Qualifier playoffs, Team Nemesis faces REKONIX in a pivotal BO3 lower bracket final rematch after Nemesis' dominant 2-0 upper quarterfinal win over REKONIX on April 12. Nemesis advanced with another 2-0 over Carstensz Esports but fell 1-2 to Ivory in the upper final yesterday, exposing potential draft vulnerabilities on patch 7.41b. REKONIX, meanwhile, surged through the lower bracket with emphatic 2-0 sweeps of Execration and OG—knocking out the experienced international roster—followed by a gritty 2-1 over GLYPH, building undeniable momentum and adaptation edge. Traders assess Nemesis' star carry 23savage and mid Mac against REKONIX's cohesive Indonesian core (Jikroy, inYourdreaM), with no roster changes impacting availability.

title: Arizona Diamondbacks vs. Baltimore Orioles

eventMetadata.context_description: Baltimore Orioles carry momentum into game two at Camden Yards following their stunning 9-7 comeback win over the Arizona Diamondbacks in Monday's opener, erasing a 6-run deficit behind Jeremiah Jackson's grand slam and Pete Alonso's go-ahead homer. Diamondbacks starter Merrill Kelly makes his 2026 debut off the injured list, facing Trevor Rogers, who enters with three consecutive quality starts for Baltimore. Both teams hover near .500 early season—D-backs 9-8, Orioles 9-7—amid key absences: Arizona without catcher Gabriel Moreno and first baseman Carlos Santana (thigh), while Baltimore misses Adley Rutschman (ankle), Ryan Mountcastle (foot fracture), and others on the injured list. Home-field edge, pitching matchup, and bullpen resilience shape trader consensus in this interleague clash.

title: LoL: Invictus Gaming vs Top Esports (BO3) - Esports World Cup China Qualifier Phase 2
description:
This market refers to the LoL Lower bracket round 1 match between Invictus Gaming and Top Esports in the Esports World Cup China Qualifier Phase 2, initially scheduled for April 13 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Invictus Gaming" if Invictus Gaming win the match against Top Esports.

This market will resolve to "Top Esports" if Top Esports win the match against Invictus Gaming.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Any Player Penta Kill?
markets.description:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Penta Kill during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Penta Kill.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether a Penta Kill occurred prior to stoppage. If no Penta Kill occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all champion kills recorded during the match by both Invictus Gaming and Top Esports. Executions (deaths to non-champion sources such as turrets, minions, or neutral monsters where no enemy champion receives kill credit) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Game 1, or if Game 1 is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

markets.description:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Invictus Gaming and Top Esports each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 2. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 2.

markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 2. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Invictus Gaming and Top Esports each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 2. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 2. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

markets.question: Game 3: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 3 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 3 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all champion kills recorded during the match by both Invictus Gaming and Top Esports. Executions (deaths to non-champion sources such as turrets, minions, or neutral monsters where no enemy champion receives kill credit) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Game 3, or if Game 3 is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Invictus Gaming and Top Esports each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Invictus Gaming and Top Esports each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game Handicap: IG (-1.5) vs Top Esports (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL Lower bracket round 1 match between Invictus Gaming and Top Esports in the Esports World Cup China Qualifier Phase 2, initially scheduled for April 14 at 6:45AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Invictus Gaming" if Invictus Gaming wins 2 or more games than Top Esports in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Top Esports". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 1 is 49 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Invictus Gaming holds the edge in trader consensus entering this high-stakes losers' bracket BO3 against Top Esports in Esports World Cup 2026 China Qualifier Phase 2, where the loser faces elimination. Both teams dropped from the upper bracket via 0-2 losses to Weibo Gaming and Anyone's Legend, respectively, heightening pressure in the double-elimination format. iG rides a three-series win streak in LPL Split 2 Group Ascend (6-1 games), including a decisive 2-0 sweep over TES on April 4, plus victories over Ninjas in Pyjamas (2-1) and Team WE (2-0). TES counters at 3-1 series (6-2 games) with recent wins over Team WE and JD Gaming, bolstered by historical 13-8 head-to-head lead, though iG's integration of new top laner Breathe and ADC JiaQi adds upset intrigue amid Patch 26.7 meta shifts.

title: Club Atlético de Madrid vs. FC Barcelona - More Markets
description: More markets for the UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for April 14 at 3:00 PM ET.

markets.question: Spread: Club Atlético de Madrid (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for April 14 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Club Atlético de Madrid" if Club Atlético de Madrid win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "FC Barcelona".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on uefa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Barcelona lead La Liga with a dominant 24-1-4 record and 73 points after 29 matches, holding a commanding position atop the table ahead of Real Madrid, Villarreal, and fourth-placed Atlético Madrid (17-6-6, 57 points), setting the stakes high for this Matchday 30 clash at Wanda Metropolitano on April 4. Post-international break, trader consensus reflects Barcelona's superior recent form—including a 3-0 Copa del Rey semifinal second-leg win over Atlético last month—tempered by Raphinha's confirmed five-week hamstring absence and Frenkie de Jong's doubtful status, though Alejandro Balde and Jules Koundé are expected available. Atlético boast a formidable 13-1-1 home record but face injury woes with Jan Oblak (hip/muscle), Pablo Barrios (muscle, out until April 10), Alexander Sørloth (head), and Johnny Cardoso doubtful from international duty, marking the first of three pivotal meetings in 11 days amid their push for Champions League spots.

title: West Bengal Legislative Assembly Election Winner
description:
Parliamentary elections are to be scheduled to be held in West Bengal, India, in March–April 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next West Bengal Legislative Assembly election. 

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the West Bengal Parliament. 

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Indian government, specifically the Election Commission of India (ECI) (eci.gov.in). If there are multiple reports, this market will resolve based on the one that includes the most Assembly Constituents (AC).
